sections.body_en: This is a sample brief showing the daily format. Bitcoin spent the last 24 hours consolidating in a tight range after Wednesday's push higher, while Ethereum held its ground above the level it reclaimed earlier in the week. Volumes were unremarkable — the market is waiting rather than deciding.

Structure remains constructive: the sequence of higher lows from late June is intact, and yesterday's pullback stopped exactly where buyers stepped in twice before. The level to watch above is the early-July swing high; losing the week's low would put the range back in play.

ETH continues to track BTC with slightly less energy. It is holding the reclaimed level but hasn't attracted follow-through buying. Relative strength against BTC is flat — no rotation signal either way yet.

News flow was quiet and slightly positive: continued ETF inflows and no negative regulatory headlines. Funding rates are near neutral — traders are not leaning aggressively in either direction, which usually means the next impulse sets the tone.
Weekend liquidity begins tonight: moves can stretch further than they should on thin order books. US macro data lands Tuesday — expect positioning to lighten into it.

1) BTC reaction if the early-July high is tested. 2) ETH/BTC ratio — a push higher would signal risk appetite broadening. 3) Funding rates flipping meaningfully positive, which would flag crowded longs.
